general_info.definition: Curved carbon dots (CDs) bottom- up fabricated using sputter-plasma assisted chemical vapour deposition (SPA-CVD) process

method.conditions: (a) Deposition system with radio-frequency sputtering and thermal evaporation system in ultra-high vacuum(10^-7 Torr base pressure). (b) Wafer-size Al foil and Si and BK7 glass used as substrate is placed on a rotatable stage. (c) 1-10 nm Cu is thermally evaporated (7.5 V, 65 A) onto the substrate to form a thin film, then placed in alumina crucibles. (d) C(1-16nm thickness) film is sputtered onto the Cu film to form a layered structure. (d) Annealing at 380°C for 1 h in situ simultaneously transforms the Cu film into nanoparticles, and the C film into CDs on top of Cu-NPs.

structural.morphology: Curved. Determined by the hemispherical shape of the nucleated Cu-NPs.

applications.performance: Non-volatile memory with write (V_W = +8 V), erase (V_E = -6 V), read (V_R = 0.5 V) cycles. Current difference between states: ~6.0 ± 0.5 μA at V_R.
applications.comparative_advantage: Lower fabrication cost vs. silicon-based flash memory. Tunable CD size via deposition parameters.
